family_obligation.label: Family Duty
family_obligation.description: The extent to which individuals believe in duties to family, including caring for parents and prioritizing family expectations. Higher values reflect stronger norms of familial responsibility and collectivism.

Immigration.label: Immigration
Immigration.description: Preference for prioritizing citizens over outsiders (e.g., immigrants) in economic opportunities. Higher values indicate stronger support for immigration over in-group favoritism.

gender_roles.label: Traditional Gender Roles
gender_roles.description: Beliefs about appropriate roles and capabilities of men and women in society, including leadership, work, and education. Higher values indicate more traditional, male-advantaged role preferences.

redistribution.label: Redistribution
redistribution.description: Support for reducing economic inequality through government action, including income equalization and state responsibility for welfare. Higher scores favor redistribution over individual responsibility.

market_orientation.label: Free Markets
market_orientation.description: Beliefs about the role of markets, including support for private ownership, competition, and merit-based outcomes. Higher values indicate stronger support for market-based economic systems.

environment.label: Environment
environment.description: Preferences regarding trade-offs between environmental protection and economic growth. Higher values indicate prioritization of environmental protection over economic growth.

civic_morality.label: Civic Violation Tolerance
civic_morality.description: Judgments about the acceptability of rule-breaking behaviors such as fraud, tax evasion, and corruption. Higher values indicate greater tolerance for violations of civic rules.

lifestyle_morality.label: Lifestyle Tolerance
lifestyle_morality.description: Attitudes toward personal and private behaviors such as sexuality, marriage, and end-of-life decisions. Higher values indicate greater moral permissiveness and acceptance of diverse lifestyles.

violence_punishment.label: Violence Tolerance
violence_punishment.description: Views on the acceptability of violence and punitive actions, including interpersonal violence and state-sanctioned punishment. Higher values indicate greater tolerance for violence or harsh punitive measures.

fairness.label: Electoral Integrity
fairness.description: Perceptions of whether elections are conducted fairly, including vote counting, media coverage, and voter choice. Higher values indicate stronger perceptions of electoral integrity.

corruption_coercion.label: Electoral Corruption
corruption_coercion.description: Perceptions of manipulation in elections, including bribery, elite influence, and voter intimidation. Higher values indicate higher perceived corruption.

authoritarianism.label: Authoritarianism
authoritarianism.description: Support for concentrated political power, including limiting dissent, prioritizing order, and allowing leaders to override democratic institutions. Higher values indicate stronger authoritarian preferences.

populism.label: Populism
populism.description: Beliefs that society is divided between ordinary people and corrupt elites, and that political elites are disconnected from the public. Higher values indicate stronger anti-elite, populist sentiment.

technocracy.label: Technocracy
technocracy.description: Preferences for decision-making based on expertise and evidence versus the will of the people. Higher values reflect more support for expert-driven governance and less emphasis on popular or non-expert judgment.

liberal_democracy.label: Liberal Democracy
liberal_democracy.description: Support for core democratic principles such as free elections, civil rights, and gender equality. Higher values indicate stronger endorsement of liberal democratic norms.

social_democracy.label: Social Democracy
social_democracy.description: Beliefs that democracy should include economic redistribution and social welfare protections. Higher values indicate stronger support for egalitarian and welfare-oriented policies within democracy.

authoritarian_democracy.label: Restricted Democracy
authoritarian_democracy.description: Beliefs that democracy can include strong authority, obedience, or non-democratic elements such as military intervention or religious control. Higher values indicate greater acceptance of authoritarian features within a democratic system.
